title: Mildly Charred Turkish Fish Manti
description: A light and comforting Turkish dish featuring charred fish and manti, perfect for a sick day. It is low in fat and made with wholesome ingredients.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, salt, and black pepper.
Gradually add egg and water to the bowl, kneading the mixture until a smooth dough forms.
Cover the dough with plastic wrap and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into small pieces and roll each piece into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of chopped onion and a piece of cod fillet in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a triangle or square shape, and press the edges together to seal the manti.
Heat olive oil in a large molcajete or skillet over medium heat.
Add the manti to the molcajete, leaving some space between each piece to allow for even cooking.
Cook the manti for 5-7 minutes on each side, or until they are lightly charred and cooked through.
Serve the manti hot, garnished with chopped fresh dill and a squeeze of lemon juice.
